arxiv.title: 2 OLMo 2 Furious
arxiv.abstract: We present OLMo 2, the next generation of our fully open language models. arxiv.title: LLM-MedQA: Enhancing Medical Question Answering through Case Studies in Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: Accurate and efficient question-answering systems are essential for delivering high-quality patient care in the medical field. arxiv.title: ICONS: Influence Consensus for Vision-Language Data Selection
arxiv.abstract: Training vision-language models via instruction tuning relies on large data mixtures spanning diverse tasks and domains, yet these mixtures frequently include redundant information that increases computational costs without proportional gains. arxiv.title: Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time
arxiv.abstract: Over more than a decade there has been an extensive research effort on how to effectively utilize recurrent models and attention. arxiv.title: Finding Missed Code Size Optimizations in Compilers using LLMs
arxiv.abstract: Compilers are complex, and significant effort has been expended on testing them. Techniques such as random program generation and differential testing have proved highly effective and have uncovered thousands of bugs in production compilers. arxiv.title: Efficient Standardization of Clinical Notes using Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: Clinician notes are a rich source of patient information but often contain inconsistencies due to varied writing styles, colloquialisms, abbreviations, medical jargon, grammatical errors, and non-standard formatting. arxiv.title: Why Are Positional Encodings Nonessential for Deep Autoregressive Transformers? Revisiting a Petroglyph
arxiv.abstract: Do autoregressive Transformer language models require explicit positional encodings (PEs)? The answer is 'no' provided they have more than one layer -- they can distinguish sequences with permuted tokens without the need for explicit PEs. This follows from the fact that a cascade of (permutation inv

arxiv.title: Taming Feed-forward Reconstruction Models as Latent Encoders for 3D Generative Models
arxiv.abstract: Recent AI-based 3D content creation has largely evolved along two paths: feed-forward image-to-3D reconstruction approaches and 3D generative models trained with 2D or 3D supervision. arxiv.title: Enabling New HDLs with Agents
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents are transforming the programming language landscape by facilitating learning for beginners, enabling code generation, and optimizing documentation workflows. arxiv.title: A Study on Context Length and Efficient Transformers for Biomedical Image Analysis
arxiv.abstract: Biomedical imaging modalities often produce high-resolution, multi-dimensional images that pose computational challenges for deep neural networks.
